ruleset.name: E-Cash PMD Ruleset
ruleset.description: PMD ruleset for the E-Cash payments team at Société Générale. Enforces: Clean Architecture, DDD, SOLID, DTO naming, DAO/JPA patterns, SGConnect OAuth2 security hygiene, OpenAPI documentation, and SG coding standards.

rule.name: DTOSuffixRequired
rule.message: Data Transfer Objects must use the suffix 'DTO' (e.g. PaymentDTO, not PaymentData or PaymentResponse)
rule.description: All classes in the dto package or implementing Serializable as transfer objects must use the DTO suffix. SG E-Cash standard.

rule.name: DAOSuffixRequired
rule.message: Data Access Objects must use the suffix 'DAO' (e.g. PaymentDAO, not PaymentRepository used directly as a DAO)
rule.description: Classes in the dao or persistence package must end with DAO. JPA Repositories are wrapped by DAOs — do not expose repositories directly to the application layer.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //ClassDeclaration[ ancestor::PackageDeclaration[contains(@Name,'.dao') or contains(@Name,'.persistence')] and not(ends-with(@SimpleName,'DAO')) and not(ends-with(@SimpleName,'Test')) and not(ends-with(@SimpleName,'Config')) ]

rule.description: Enforce PascalCase for all class names.
Enforce camelCase for all method names.
Enforce camelCase for fields, UPPER_SNAKE for static finals.
Package names must be lowercase.
Variable names should not exceed 40 characters.
Variable names should be at least 2 characters (except loop counters).
S in SOLID: A class doing too much is a God Class. Breaks single responsibility. Split into domain, application, and infrastructure concerns.
S in SOLID: Classes must not exceed 200 lines. Extract responsibilities.
S in SOLID: Methods must not exceed 30 lines. Extract private helpers or domain services.
S in SOLID: Methods with more than 4 parameters suggest the method is doing too much. Use a command/request DTO instead.
S in SOLID: Classes with more than 10 fields likely have too many responsibilities.
S in SOLID: Classes with more than 15 public methods violate single responsibility.
O in SOLID: High cyclomatic complexity signals switch/if chains that should be replaced with polymorphism (strategy pattern, open/closed principle).
O in SOLID: High NPath complexity — replace conditionals with polymorphism.
D in SOLID: Too many concrete type dependencies. Depend on abstractions (interfaces). Especially important at the infrastructure layer boundary.

rule.name: NoDomainDependencyOnInfrastructure
rule.message: Domain classes must not import from infrastructure or persistence packages. Domain layer must remain pure.
rule.description: Clean Architecture: The domain layer must have zero outward dependencies. No JPA, no Spring Data, no message brokers in the domain package.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //ImportDeclaration[ ancestor::CompilationUnit[ PackageDeclaration[contains(@Name,'.domain')] ] and ( contains(@Name,'javax.persistence') or contains(@Name,'jakarta.persistence') or contains(@Name,'org.springframework.data') or contains(@Name,'org.springframework.amqp') or contains(@Name,'org.springframework.kafka') or contains(@Name,'.infrastructure.') or contains(@Name,'.persistence.') or contains(@Name,'.repository.') ) ]

rule.name: NoJPAAnnotationsInDomain
rule.message: JPA annotations (@Entity, @Table, @Column) must not appear in the domain layer. Use infrastructure adapters.
rule.description: Domain entities must not be JPA entities. Use separate persistence adapters that map between domain models and JPA entities.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: NoDirectRepositoryInApplicationLayer
rule.message: Application services must not inject Spring Data Repositories directly. Use DAO interfaces defined in the domain/application layer.
rule.description: Spring Data Repositories are an infrastructure concern. Application services must depend on DAO interfaces, not repository implementations.

rule.name: ControllersMustNotHaveBusinessLogic
rule.message: Controllers must only delegate to application services. No business logic, no direct DB access.
rule.description: Presentation layer controllers must be thin. Any logic beyond request mapping, validation delegation, and response mapping is a violation.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: TransactionalRequiredOnDAOMutations
rule.message: DAO methods that modify data (save, update, delete) must be annotated with @Transactional.
rule.description: All write operations in DAO classes must be transactional to ensure data consistency. In E-Cash payment flows, partial writes without transactions cause reconciliation issues.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //MethodDeclaration[ ancestor::ClassDeclaration[ends-with(@SimpleName,'DAO')] and ( starts-with(@Name,'save') or starts-with(@Name,'update') or starts-with(@Name,'delete') or starts-with(@Name,'insert') or starts-with(@Name,'persist') or starts-with(@Name,'merge') or starts-with(@Name,'remove') or starts-with(@Name,'create') ) and not(.//Annotation[@SimpleName='Transactional']) ]

rule.name: NoRawJPAInServiceLayer
rule.message: Service classes must not use EntityManager directly. Encapsulate JPA access in the DAO layer.
rule.description: EntityManager usage must be confined to the DAO/persistence layer. Application services work through DAO interfaces only.

rule.name: NoOpenTransactionInPresentation
rule.message: @Transactional must not be used in controllers or presentation layer classes. Transactions belong in the DAO/application layer.
rule.description: Open sessions in view and transaction management in controllers is an anti-pattern. All transaction boundaries must be in the application or DAO layer.
JPA resources must be properly closed to avoid connection leaks in payment flows.

rule.name: NoHardcodedSecurityCredentials
rule.message: Hardcoded client IDs, secrets, or tokens detected. SGConnect credentials must come from centralised configuration (Vault / Spring Cloud Config), never from source code.
rule.description: SGConnect OAuth2 client_id, client_secret, and tokens must never be hardcoded. All credentials must be injected via @Value from a centralised config server or Vault.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //VariableDeclarator[ ( contains(lower-case(@Name),'secret') or contains(lower-case(@Name),'clientid') or contains(lower-case(@Name),'client_id') or contains(lower-case(@Name),'token') or contains(lower-case(@Name),'password') or contains(lower-case(@Name),'apikey') or contains(lower-case(@Name),'api_key') ) and StringLiteral[string-length(@Image) > 0] ]

rule.name: NoHardcodedSGConnectUrls
rule.message: SGConnect authorization server URL must not be hardcoded. Use centralised configuration: sgconnect.auth-server-url in application.yml.
rule.description: The SGConnect OAuth2 authorization server URL changes between environments. It must be externalised to configuration, never hardcoded in source.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: SecurityConfigMustNotPermitAll
rule.message: permitAll() detected in SecurityConfig. All E-Cash endpoints must be protected by SGConnect OAuth2 scopes. Review this deliberately.
rule.description: In E-Cash payment APIs, open endpoints are a security risk. Any use of permitAll() must be explicitly justified in a comment.

rule.name: NoHardcodedPortNumbers
rule.message: Hardcoded port numbers detected. All ports must be externalised to application.yml / Spring Cloud Config.
rule.description: Port numbers, hostnames, and connection strings must come from centralised configuration. Hardcoding causes environment drift and duplicated config.

property.name: xpath

rule.description: Duplicate string literals indicate missing constants. Centralise repeated values as named constants or externalise to config.
Fields that are never modified should be final. Immutable configuration values are safer in concurrent payment processing.

rule.name: ControllersMustHaveOpenAPIAnnotation
rule.message: REST controllers must be annotated with @Tag (OpenAPI). All E-Cash APIs must be documented with Swagger OAS3.
rule.description: Every controller must have @Tag for grouping in the API portal. Each endpoint method must have @Operation with summary and description.

rule.name: EndpointsMustHaveOperationAnnotation
rule.message: REST endpoint methods must have @Operation(summary, description) for OpenAPI documentation.
rule.description: All public endpoint methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) must be documented with @Operation so they appear correctly in the E-Cash API portal.

rule.name: DTOsMustHaveSchemaAnnotation
rule.message: DTO classes must use @Schema to document their fields for OpenAPI. Consumers need to understand the payment data model.
rule.description: All DTO classes used in request/response bodies must have @Schema annotations on the class and on each field to produce meaningful OpenAPI documentation.

rule.name: NoMockitoOnDomainClasses
rule.message: Mockito.mock() must not be used on domain classes. Domain objects must be instantiated directly. Mock only external system boundaries.
rule.description: Mocking domain classes hides real behaviour. Only infrastructure boundaries (external APIs, message brokers, third-party payment gateways) should be mocked.

property.name: xpath

rule.description: Test methods must contain at least one assertion. Tests with no assertions give false confidence in payment flows.
Tests with too many assertions are testing multiple behaviours. Split into focused tests.
Empty catch blocks silently swallow payment errors. Every exception must be logged and handled or rethrown.
Catching Exception or Throwable is too broad. Catch specific payment exceptions.
Never return null for collections in payment APIs. Return empty collections. Prevents NullPointerExceptions in payment processing chains.
Use isEmpty() instead of size() == 0 for collection checks.
Avoid unnecessary String instantiation in high-throughput payment loops.
In payment processing loops, use StringBuilder instead of string concatenation.
Prefer fine-grained synchronization blocks over method-level synchronization in concurrent payment processing.
Public APIs, domain classes, and DAO interfaces must have Javadoc.
